title: MATRINE
description: Matrine is a tetracyclic quinolizidine alkaloid extracted from the medicinal plant Sophora flavescens that exhibits anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective, and anti-proliferative properties. It works by modulating multiple cellular signaling pathways including TLR4/NF-κB/MAPK, NLRP3 inflammasome, and AMPK pathways. Traditionally used in Chinese medicine, matrine has been studied for its effects on liver diseases, inflammation, neurological disorders, and cancer.

research.title: Matrine exerts its neuroprotective effects by modulating multiple neuronal pathways
research.summary: Comprehensive review demonstrating that matrine protects neurons by crossing the blood-brain barrier and modulating multiple signaling pathways, showing therapeutic potential for Multiple Sclerosis, Alzheimer's disease, and other neurological disorders.

research.title: Matrine Exerts Pharmacological Effects Through Multiple Signaling Pathways: A Comprehensive Review
research.summary: Detailed review showing matrine has broad pharmacological activities including anti-tumor, anti-inflammatory, analgesic, anti-fibrotic, anti-viral effects through activation or inhibition of key molecules in cellular signaling pathways, with therapeutic effects in various tumors, cardiopathy, and CNS inflammation.

research.title: Preventive effects of matrine on LPS-induced inflammation in RAW 264.7 cells and intestinal damage in mice through the TLR4/NF-κB/MAPK pathway
research.summary: Study demonstrates matrine's anti-inflammatory properties by inhibiting the TLR4/NF-κB/MAPK pathway, showing protective effects against LPS-induced intestinal damage and inflammation in both cell and mouse models.

research.title: Matrine suppresses NLRP3 inflammasome activation via regulating PTPN2/JNK/SREBP2 pathway in sepsis
research.summary: Research showing matrine suppresses abnormal NLRP3 inflammasome activation in sepsis through the PTPN2/JNK/SREBP2 pathway, demonstrating anti-inflammatory properties in life-threatening dysregulated host responses to infection.

research.title: Oxymatrine relieves non-alcoholic fatty liver disease by promoting sirtuin 1/adenosine 5'-monophosphate-activated protein kinase pathway and peroxisome proliferator activated receptor alpha-mediated hepatic fatty acid oxidation
research.summary: Study demonstrates that oxymatrine (a derivative of matrine) has protective effects in NAFLD by promoting the SIRT1/AMPK pathway and enhancing hepatic fatty acid oxidation, showing therapeutic potential for liver disease.

research.title: Matrine inhibits vascular smooth muscle cell proliferation by modulating the expression of cell cycle regulatory genes
research.summary: Research showing matrine inhibits vascular smooth muscle cell proliferation in a dose-dependent manner by promoting G1 arrest through up-regulation of p53, p21, and p27, demonstrating potential cardiovascular benefits.

research.title: Effects of oxymatrine and matrine on left ventricular contractility using pressure-volume relationship analysis in anesthetized rats
research.summary: Study using advanced cardiac analysis showed that both oxymatrine and matrine significantly improved load-independent cardiac parameters, increased cardiac efficiency, and improved mechanoenergetics, suggesting cardiovascular benefits.

research.title: Matrine induces ferroptosis in cervical cancer through activation of piezo1 channel
research.summary: Research demonstrates matrine's anti-cancer properties by inducing ferroptosis (a form of cell death) in cervical cancer cells through activation of the piezo1 channel, showing potential therapeutic utility in cancer treatment.

sentiment.summary: User experiences are limited and primarily negative, with one individual reporting anxiety and derealization from 100mg dose, expressing concern about potential 5-AR inhibition. The user data shows no positive user testimonials for matrine itself, with discussions focused on other supplements entirely.

effects.label: Research-Backed:
effects.description: Extensive preclinical research demonstrates anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective, and anti-proliferative effects across multiple disease models

effects.label: Multiple Pathways:
effects.description: Works through various cellular signaling mechanisms rather than a single target, potentially explaining broad pharmacological activities

effects.label: Traditional Use:
effects.description: Long history of use in Traditional Chinese Medicine for liver conditions and inflammation

effects.label: Limited Human Data:
effects.description: Most evidence comes from cell culture and animal studies; human clinical trial data remains limited

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Animal studies typically use 50-200 mg/kg, though human equivalent doses not well established

dosage.label: User Experience:
dosage.description: One Reddit user reported negative effects at 100mg dose in humans

dosage.label: Extract Standardization:
dosage.description: Commercial products often use 98% matrine extracts from Sophora flavescens

dosage.label: Traditional Preparation:
dosage.description: Historically consumed as part of whole plant preparations rather than isolated compound

sideEffects.label: Psychological Effects:
sideEffects.description: User report of anxiety and derealization at 100mg dose

sideEffects.label: 5-AR Inhibition Concern:
sideEffects.description: Potential 5-alpha reductase inhibitory activity suspected in parent plant, though not confirmed for isolated matrine

sideEffects.label: Kappa-Opioid Activity:
sideEffects.description: Known kappa-opioid receptor activity may contribute to dysphoric effects

sideEffects.label: Safety Profile:
sideEffects.description: Research papers generally note matrine as well-tolerated in animal studies, but human safety data limited

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Available as isolated extract (98% matrine) from supplement companies like reputable suppliers

availability.label: Plant Source:
availability.description: Derived from Sophora flavescens (Ku Shen) and related Sophora species used in Traditional Chinese Medicine

availability.label: Research Chemical:
availability.description: Primarily used in research settings; consumer supplement use appears minimal based on user discussions
